title: heatwave red alert remains in chhatarpur with temperatures above 41°C - The News Mill
search_term: heatwave ("Agar Malwa" OR Alirajpur OR Anuppur OR Ashoknagar OR Balaghat OR Barwani OR Betul OR Bhind OR Bhopal OR Burhanpur OR Chhatarpur OR Chhindwara OR Damoh OR Datia OR Dewas OR Dhar OR Dindori OR Guna OR Gwalior OR Harda OR Hoshangabad OR Indore OR Jabalpur OR Jhabua OR Katni OR Khandwa OR Khargone OR Mandla OR Mandsaur OR Morena OR Narsinghpur OR Neemuch OR Panna OR Raisen OR Rajgarh OR Ratlam OR Rewa OR Sagar OR Satna OR Sehore OR Seoni OR Shahdol OR Shajapur OR Sheopur OR Shivpuri OR Sidhi OR Singrauli OR Tikamgarh OR Ujjain OR Umaria OR Vidisha) Madhya Pradesh
full_text:
A red alert for heatwaves continues in Chhatarpur district, Madhya Pradesh, as temperatures have surpassed 41°C since early on Sunday, May 24. Nearby areas such as Khajuraho and Nowgong have experienced temperatures as high as 47.4°C in recent days, with severe heatwave conditions persisting.
The extreme heat has led to deserted local markets as residents remain indoors, stepping outside only for essential activities. Many are attempting to mitigate the effects of the intense heat by drinking water and sugarcane juice.
Dilip Kshatriya, a local resident, said, “There is a continuous red alert for a heatwave in the district. If we talk about the temperatures, it has crossed 41°C since early morning, making it extremely difficult for people to step out of their houses. People are only leaving their homes for highly essential or urgent work.”
He also stated, “The severity of the heatwave is such that a complete silence has taken over the local weekly markets (haat-bazaars). To protect themselves from this scorching weather, people are continuously drinking water and sugarcane juice to quench their thirst. The severe impact of the weather can be felt everywhere.”
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast that temperatures in the national capital will reach 44°C today. The IMD advises the public to avoid prolonged exposure to direct sunlight, stay hydrated, and limit outdoor activities during peak afternoon hours.
Recommendations include consuming seasonal fruits such as watermelon, cucumber, and cantaloupe, as well as leafy vegetables. The IMD suggests drinking shikanji and lassi instead of caffeine, alcohol, and sugary beverages to help maintain lower body temperatures.
Many residents have adopted protective measures, with people in urban areas commonly using umbrellas, wide-brimmed hats, and fabric scarves to shield themselves from direct solar radiation while outdoors.

title: 'Nautapa' begins across country as heatwave intensifies: Maharashtra's Brahmapuri records 47.2°C, mercury c... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
- Brahmapuri 47.2°C | MP UP Cities Mercury Crosses 45°C; Nautapa Heatwave Intensifies
'Nautapa' begins across country as heatwave intensifies:Maharashtra's Brahmapuri records 47.2°C, mercury crosses 45°C in 8 MP-UP cities
- Copy link
Nautapa has begun across the country today, meaning the next nine days are likely to be even hotter. Heatwave conditions and temperatures are expected to rise further, with temperatures possibly crossing 45°C.
Brahmapuri, in Maharashtra’s Vidarbha region, was the hottest city in the country for the third straight day on Sunday, recording 47.2°C, which was 4.6°C above normal.
Banda was the hottest district in Uttar Pradesh, recording a high of 46.8°C. Prayagraj and Orai recorded 45.6°C, while Jhansi reached 45.6°C, Agra 45.4°C and Hamirpur 45.2°C.
In Madhya Pradesh, Naugaon and Khajuraho recorded the highest temperature at 45.8°C. According to the Meteorological Department, the weather in northern and central India may change from May 29, with rain likely.
Read these two reports on staying healthy during Nautapa
1. Intense heat during Nautapa can increase the risk of illness: Know from doctors what to do and what to avoid during these 9 days
Nautapa begins across the country today. These nine days directly affect our health as temperatures and heatwave conditions become more intense. The heat can affect everything from the heart to the brain. Therefore, it is important to make small changes to your daily routine and diet during Nautapa.
2. Risk of nosebleeds rises in summer: Know what to do and what to avoid if your nose starts bleeding
High summer temperatures can make the air dry. When breathing, this dry air can irritate the nasal mucus lining. In some cases, this may cause blood vessels to rupture, leading to nosebleeds. What should be done immediately if a nosebleed occurs? Who is more at risk?
Weather Forecast for the Next Two Days
May 26:
Heatwave conditions are expected in Haryana, Delhi, Punjab, Rajasthan, Chhattisgarh, Himachal Pradesh, Jammu and Kashmir, Jharkhand, Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh. Humid weather will continue to trouble West Bengal and Odisha.
Heavy rain alerts have been issued for several states, including Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, Bihar, Kerala, Lakshadweep and Tamil Nadu. Thunderstorms and strong winds are possible in Bihar. Thunderstorm alerts have also been issued for Andhra Pradesh, Jharkhand, Odisha, Telangana, Karnataka and West Bengal.
May 27:
Heatwave conditions are likely in Chhattisgarh, Rajasthan, Himachal Pradesh, Jammu and Kashmir, Odisha, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Delhi, Madhya Pradesh, Punjab and Maharashtra.
Heavy rain alerts remain in place for Bihar, Kerala, Assam and Meghalaya. Strong winds with speeds of up to 50–60 kmph are also expected.
Andhra Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Odisha and Telangana may receive rain along with strong winds of 40–50 kmph and lightning.
Weather photos from across the country…
Weather across States
Madhya Pradesh: Heatwave to prevail in 44 districts of the state, red alert in Niwari-Tikamgarh, Chhatarpur-Panna and Satna
Nautapa begins today in the state and will continue until June 2nd. According to the Meteorological Department, the entire state will be affected by a heatwave from May 25th to 28th. A heatwave alert is in effect today in 44 districts. A red heatwave alert is in effect in five districts: Niwari, Tikamgarh, Chhatarpur, Panna, and Satna.
Uttar Pradesh: Heatwave red alert issued for 10 districts; Banda second hottest city in the country, with a temperature of 46.8°C
The Meteorological Department has issued a red alert for heatwave in 10 districts and an orange alert for 23 districts for Monday. A storm and rain warning has been issued for 14 districts in Purvanchal. Banda, with a temperature of 46.8°C on Sunday, was the second hottest city in the country.
Rajasthan: Temperatures could exceed 47°C, heatwave alert issued for 19 districts today, rain and hailstorms expected
Today, three districts in Rajasthan have issued an orange heatwave alert, and 16 districts have issued a yellow heatwave alert. Maximum temperatures could reach 47°C in the next two to three days. A western disturbance is expected to become active after May 28th. Due to this, rain and hailstorms are likely in some districts.
Bihar: Heatwave alert issued in 14 districts today, thunderstorm and rain warning in 13 cities
An orange heatwave alert has been issued for 14 districts in Bihar today. Additionally, an orange thunderstorm alert has been issued for 13 districts. Winds could reach speeds of up to 50 kmph in these districts. On Sunday, temperatures in nine districts of Bihar exceeded 40°C. The highest temperature was 44.8°C in Dehri (Rohtas).
Punjab: Orange alert for heatwave in Punjab-Chandigarh, school and office timings changed from today
A heatwave alert has been issued in Punjab and Chandigarh for the next three days, starting today. Due to rising temperatures, the Punjab government has changed the timings of government offices and schools. In Chandigarh, schools will remain closed until June 30th. Outdoor activities at school summer camps have also been banned.
Uttarakhand: Dehradun breaks 7-year heat record, Auli-Chakrata reaches 31°C; rain expected in 5 districts
The maximum temperature in Dehradun, Uttarakhand, reached 40.4°C on May 24th. Seven years ago, in 2019, the temperature also reached 40.4°C on the same date. Kashipur and Jaspur were the hottest cities in the state, with temperatures reaching 44°C. Hill stations Auli and Chakrata also recorded maximum temperatures of 31°C.
